question.text: Under Regulation 13(3) of SEBI LODR 2015, within how many days from the end of each quarter must a listed entity submit the statement of investor complaints?

answer.text: Within 15 days of the quarter end
Within 21 days of the quarter end
Within 30 days of the quarter end
Within 45 days of the quarter end

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 13(3) of SEBI LODR requires listed entities to submit the statement of investor complaints within 30 days from the end of each quarter. This filing tracks the number of complaints received, resolved, and pending. **The Trap:** The 21-day timeline is for shareholding pattern filing (Reg 31), and 45-day timeline is for financial results (Reg 33). Candidates often mix up these quarterly deadlines. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-A requires RTAs to understand these compliance calendars as they support the listed entity in preparing investor complaint data. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain a compliance calendar with all LODR quarterly deadlines. The investor complaints statement at 30 days, shareholding pattern at 21 days, and financial results at 45 days are the three most commonly tested timelines.
